fundamentals.name: Starbucks Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Starbucks Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a roaster, marketer, and retailer of coffee internationally. The company operates through three segments: North America, International, and Channel Development. Its stores offer coffee, tea, and other beverages, roasted whole beans and ground coffees, complementary food, packaged coffees, single-serve products, and ready-to-drink beverages; and various food products, such as pastries, breakfast sandwiches, and lunch items. The company also licenses its trademarks through licensed stores, and grocery and foodservice accounts. The company offers its products under the Starbucks Coffee, Teavana, Seattle's Best Coffee, Ethos, and Starbucks Reserve brands. Starbucks Corporation was founded in 1971 and is based in Seattle, Washington.

judgment.summary: Starbucks shows strong earnings growth and analyst optimism, but faces headwinds from high valuation, declining margins, and negative free cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $0.50 vs $0.44; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Earnings growth 32.6% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 33.0% indicate strong profitability.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (30.2%) and fiscal year (12.2%) are robust.
Mean analyst target of $106.58 suggests potential upside from the current $102.10 price.
Dividend yield of 2.5% offers a modest income stream.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 77.94 and Forward P/E of 33.71 indicate a high valuation.
Profit margin of 3.9% and operating margin of 8.4% are relatively low.
Free cash flow is negative at $-1.30B TTM, raising concerns about cash generation.
Dividend payout ratio of 187.8% is unsustainable.
Current ratio of 0.92 and quick ratio of 0.26 suggest potential short-term liquidity issues.

news.paragraph: Starbucks is ending its AI inventory program across North American stores due to inaccuracies, a move that follows a blunt message from the CEO regarding consumer spending and a recent analyst report highlighting the company's 'Bull of the Day' status. While the AI program discontinuation may raise questions about technological adoption, the company's overall analyst sentiment remains positive with a 'buy' consensus. The market will be watching for signs of consumer resilience ahead of the next earnings report.

headlines.title: Reuters: Starbucks Ends AI Inventory Program across North American Stores
